title: meshd: re-assert backhaul 10.254.x address when the backhaul iface bounces (TR3000 lost it silently)
description: Found on the live fleet 2026-07-01: TR3000 had meshd RUNNING and babeld up, mesh L2 plink ESTAB, but br-mesh had NO 10.254.x address — so babel never routed it and the node was invisible at L3 (client /24 still gossiped, creating a route to a gateway that could not answer). meshd assigns the derived backhaul address once at startup; if the interface/carrier bounces afterwards (wifi reload, oaq recovery, netifd ifdown/ifup — br-mesh addresses are flushed on down), the address is gone and meshd never notices. service mjolnir-meshd restart fixed it instantly. Fix: meshd should watch (rtnetlink addr events on backhaul_iface, or periodic check) and re-assert the address, same spirit as the babel-config reconciler. Relates: oaq (the bounce that likely flushed it), auu (dual-address flagging already exists in diag — extend to missing-address), 6e5 (mjolnir-apply health gate would catch this class post-update once nodes have baselines).
notes: MITIGATION SHIPPED in mjolnir-apply (not the daemon fix): after any wifi bounce the applier now restarts a running meshd so the backhaul addr is re-asserted before the health gate. Proven live 2026-07-01 on the TR3000 full update: pre-apply baseline (2 peers, targets 10.254.242.172 + 10.254.242.84), wifi reload, meshd restart, gate PASSED — without the restart this run would have spuriously rolled back. The daemon-side rtnetlink re-assert is still the real fix (any wifi bounce OUTSIDE mjolnir-apply still silently kills L3).

title: in-band node ops: staged payload + detached apply with health-gated rollback
description: install-node.sh currently REQUIRES ethernet/out-of-band because the wpad-basic->wpad-mesh swap and setup-wireless.sh bounce the wifi you'd be SSHing over, and the node's only collision-free address is the derived 10.254.x. Fix: split deploy into STAGE (scp binary+init+config+applier, prefetch .apk/.ipk packages on-node while connectivity is good — BOTH wpad variants so rollback never needs internet) and DETACHED APPLY (on-node /usr/sbin/mjolnir-apply run under setsid, survives SSH cut): snapshot uci configs + previous binary + installed wpad variant, apply idempotently (skip wpad swap if wpad-mesh already installed => re-runs stop bouncing wifi), then HEALTH GATE: pre-apply it records responding 10.254.x neighbours + mesh station count; post-apply it requires a mesh peer back OR a ping to a recorded neighbour within timeout, else AUTO-ROLLBACK (restore configs, previous binary, previous wpad from the prefetched pkg, wifi reload) and write result file either way; install-node.sh polls the result file over reconnecting SSH. Commit-confirm pattern (Cisco commit confirmed / LuCI uci rollback). Ethernet at 192.168.1.1 demotes to recovery-of-last-resort. Unblocks updating radio-only nodes for buw.7 multi-hop validation. Relates: buw.7, buw.9 (CRDT list = future dynamic inventory), qz9 (detached+supervised beats in-band synchronous).
notes: IMPLEMENTED (harness-verified, hardware-pending). New: deploy/openwrt/files/usr/sbin/mjolnir-apply (on-node detached applier: snapshot -> idempotent apply -> health gate -> auto-rollback; installs itself to /usr/sbin for future OTA reuse — 0kx). Rewritten: install-node.sh = stage (scp payload + on-node pkg prefetch with pkg-cache/ push/pull fallback for no-WAN boxes, exactly the case hit provisioning 2b26d886 today) + detached apply via setsid + result polling over reconnecting SSH. Re-runs no longer bounce wifi (wpad swap skipped once done). VERIFIED in unshare mount-ns harness with stubbed apk/uci/iw/wifi/ping: (1) fresh no-WAN install from prefetched pkgs, gate skipped; (2) wpad-swap + binary change with mesh NOT returning -> full rollback (configs, binary, previous wpad from prefetched pkg, wifi reload, meshd restart), exit 1; (3) healthy re-run fully non-disruptive (wifi_touched=0). REMAINING before close: first in-band run against a live node (real apk fetch flags on 25.12.4, setsid-under-dropbear survival across the actual wifi bounce, health gate over real babel routes). Wireless env files passed via --wireless contain MESH_KEY — keep them out of git.
close_reason: Hardware-validated on a fresh Cudy WR3000S v1 (25.12.4, NO WAN — the exact target scenario): stage + on-node prefetch failed to feeds -> pkg-cache push kicked in (cache filled on the workstation from downloads.openwrt.org: babeld, kmod-tun, both wpad variants incl. the kernel-keyed kmods subfeed), detached apply installed everything offline, wpad swap + binary + template OK, gate correctly skipped (fresh node, no baseline). Second run with --wireless: wpad swap SKIPPED (no bounce, idempotency confirmed on hardware), setup-wireless applied detached. Two fixes landed from the run: apk v3 sideload needs --allow-untrusted (OpenWrt signs the repo INDEX, not individual .apk files — verified even authentic feed packages fail otherwise; integrity = HTTPS->cache + SSH->node), and mkdir /etc/mjolnir so 'mjolnir-meshd id' works pre-service-start. NOT yet exercised live: the health-gate ROLLBACK on hardware (harness-only) — first genuinely in-band over-the-mesh update will prove it; acceptable residual.

title: buw: walking-skeleton validation — 2-node ping across internet, then 3-4 node multi-hop over overlay
description: End-to-end proof. Step A: 2 nodes, single TUN each, 1 iroh conn, babel over mjolnir0, ping across the REAL internet. Step B: 3-4 nodes with one transit-ONLY node to prove multi-hop OVER the overlay (kernel forwards). Confirms mux/demux + multicast-emulation + babel render together and that the qz9 wedge does not recur.
acceptance_criteria: Cross-internet ping succeeds over mjolnir0; transit node forwards; no per-peer TUN; qz9 wedge does not recur.
notes: PARTIAL PROGRESS 2026-07-01: the LAN-mode radio leg is now hardware-proven — 3-node 802.11s island (WR3000S freshie 10.254.242.84, M3000 10.254.242.172, TR3000 10.254.61.115) with babel routing over br-mesh, pings 1-17ms, after clearing two blockers: oaq (concurrent 2.4 AP kills WR3000S mesh join; CLIENT_AP_2G now defaults 0) and new bug bov (TR3000 had silently lost its backhaul addr; meshd restart re-asserted). What buw.7 still needs: the OVERLAY leg — mjolnir0 across an internet/iroh hop (2-node ping cross-internet, then 3-4 node multi-hop with a transit-only node). Node 2b26d886... (10.254.12.214) was not on the air during this session. Fleet mgmt note: nodes are reachable in-band now (ssh to 10.254.x via any node as ProxyJump; fe80..%br-mesh works for a node that lost its v4 addr).

title: buw: FIB mirror + packet mux/demux (rtnetlink route sync -> LPM trie; mjolnir0<->iroh datagrams)
description: The data plane. tun/encap.rs spawn_encap_pair becomes mux/demux: read pkt from mjolnir0 -> LPM(dest)->next-hop 10.254.x -> peer conn -> one iroh datagram; recv on ANY conn -> write pkt to mjolnir0 (kernel forwards via ip_forward=1 for multi-hop over overlay). FIB mirror: subscribe rtnetlink RTM_NEWROUTE/DELROUTE on mjolnir0, maintain dest-prefix->next-hop LPM trie (treebitmap/ip_network_table; rtnetlink already a dep). LPM-miss -> drop + rate-limited warn. Kernel ip_forward + IP TTL terminate transit loops (no custom hop logic).
acceptance_criteria: Unicast pkt from mjolnir0 reaches the correct peer via LPM; inbound writes to mjolnir0; a transit-only node forwards (multi-hop over overlay proven).
notes: Close-reason note (prior reason mangled by shell backticks): FIB mirror polls the mjolnir0 route table via ip -4 route show and parses via-gateway lines into tun::fib. All library pieces unit-tested; daemon builds + clippy clean; single-node overlay smoke PASS.
close_reason: FIB mirror + mux/demux DONE + tested. tun::fib (LPM route table, 6 unit tests). tun::overlay::spawn_overlay_routed + UnicastRouter seam (multicast floods, unicast routes to one peer, unroutable dropped — 6 overlay tests). meshd OverlayRouter impls UnicastRouter over FIB+ConnManager (v4 via FIB or on-link 10.254/16; v6 fe80::X -> 10.254.X). fib_mirror polls  -> Fib. overlay_reader drives it with a live flood set. Daemon builds + clippy clean; single-node smoke PASS (mjolnir0 up 10.254.130.188 / fe80::82bc, correct derived LL). Data path proven end-to-end in buw.1.

title: buw: babeld renders ONE static mjolnir0 interface (kills per-peer config churn / qz9)
description: babel_reconciler (meshd.rs:1057) renders per-peer tunnel ifaces from TunnelRegistry -> config churns as peers come/go (root of qz9). Render ONE static mjolnir0 interface, always present; per-peer ifaces vanish. babel/config.rs render_babeld_conf: collapse per-mj-peer 'type tunnel' lines to a single mjolnir0 iface. Metric: type tunnel + enable-timestamps + rtt-min/rtt-max/max-rtt-penalty/rtt-decay (RTT metric auto-deprioritises degraded/relay paths), NOT fixed rxcost.
acceptance_criteria: babeld.conf static across peer churn; RTT metric active; ZERO procd restarts on peer add/remove (qz9 regression check).
notes: buw.1 spike learning (IMPORTANT): the babeld render for mjolnir0 MUST deny redistribution/acceptance of the overlay-link block 10.254.0.0/16 (in/out ip 10.254.0.0/16 deny) IN ADDITION to the existing 10.255.0.0/16 deny, and keep 'redistribute local deny' + the 'redistribute deny' catch-all so ONLY the claimed client /24 is advertised. In the spike, redistributing node addresses caused babeld to install an 'unreachable <peer>/32' that was more specific than the transport's on-link route and poisoned it. The production renderer (config.rs) already denies 10.255/16 and non-claimed routes; extend the deny to the 10.254/16 overlay block when it becomes the babel interface. Metric: type tunnel + enable-timestamps + rtt-* (per bead design).
close_reason:
Renderer DONE + config validated end-to-end (2026-06-30). Added babel::config::render_overlay_babeld_conf(iface, local_subnet, OverlayRtt) — renders ONE static 'interface mjolnir0 type tunnel enable-timestamps true rtt-min/rtt-max/max-rtt-penalty/rtt-decay' with the RTT metric, redistributes ONLY the claimed client /24, and denies both the overlay-link (10.254/16) and legacy TUN-link (10.255/16) blocks in+out plus 'redistribute local deny'. OverlayRtt struct with Default {10,200,96,42}. 4 golden/property unit tests (89 lib tests pass, clippy clean).

VALIDATED: ran the buw.1 harness with IFACE_OPTS='type tunnel ... enable-timestamps true rtt-min 10 rtt-max 200 max-rtt-penalty 96 rtt-decay 42' -> PASS: full adjacency (reach ffff, cost 96), client /24 propagated over the single overlay TUN, AND the RTT metric is live (neighbour shows 'rtt 4.7 rttcost 0' — timestamps working; rttcost 0 because 4.7ms < rtt-min, correct). So type tunnel + timestamps forms adjacency over the emulated-multicast overlay and activates RTT path selection.

Static-across-churn property: the renderer takes NO peer list, so the file is byte-identical regardless of which peers are up -> nothing for procd to restart on (the qz9 root cause). The 'ZERO procd restarts on churn' acceptance is fully realized once meshd calls this renderer instead of the per-peer one on peer-set changes — that swap lands with the buw.3/buw.4 meshd integration (the reconciler stops regenerating on churn). Renderer + its output are proven here.

title: buw: single overlay TUN mjolnir0 bring-up (10.254.x/16, MTU 1300)
description: Replace per-peer tunnel bring-up (tun/iface.rs spawn_tunnel + struct Tunnel, Drop=abort) with ONE mjolnir0 TUN carrying this node's backhaul_addr(node_id)=10.254.<blake3>/16 (link.rs:39), MTU=TUNNEL_MTU(1300). One TUN for the node lifetime; no per-peer create/destroy. 10.255/16 LINK_BLOCK + pick_link_31 become unused.
acceptance_criteria: meshd brings up mjolnir0 on start, addr in 10.254/16, MTU 1300; survives peer churn; `ip link` shows exactly one overlay TUN.
notes: buw.1 spike learning: a TUN does NOT have the MULTICAST interface flag by default — babeld will not send Hellos to ff02::1:6 without it. mjolnir0 bring-up MUST set it (ip link set dev mjolnir0 multicast on, or the rtnetlink equivalent IFF_MULTICAST). Also enable IPv6 on the iface and assign a distinct fe80:: link-local (babel transports over fe80::). Confirmed working in the spike.
close_reason:
Library primitive DONE + verified on real kernel (2026-06-30). Added tun::iface::spawn_overlay_tun(node_id, iface): creates one mjolnir0 async TUN (MTU 1300), assigns the node's backhaul /16 (10.254.x via link::backhaul_addr), enables IPv6 + a derived deterministic link-local fe80::<host16> (overlay_link_local), sets the link UP and — critically — the IFF_MULTICAST flag (via rtnetlink LinkFlags::Multicast; a TUN lacks it by default and babeld won't emit Hellos to ff02::1:6 without it, per buw.1). Returns (tun::AsyncDevice, OverlayLink{iface,self_addr,link_local,index}); dropping the device removes the iface. Also OVERLAY_IFACE const.

VERIFIED: ignored root test overlay_tun_up_with_multicast_flag PASSES — asserts the kernel iface is UP with IFF_MULTICAST set and a 10.254/16 addr. Pure unit test overlay_link_local_is_stable_and_distinct passes. clippy clean; no new warnings (uses tun_name() not the deprecated name()).

The meshd startup call-site (bring mjolnir0 up on boot) is a one-liner that lands naturally with buw.3 (connection manager creates the overlay at startup) + buw.4 (demux drives it). Primitive is the shared foundation for buw.3/4/5.

NOTE: found a pre-existing broken ignored test create_and_destroy_real_tun (PeerInterface::create drops its sync tun device so close() hits ENODEV) — unrelated to buw; left as-is.

title: buw: connection manager — EndpointId<->iroh Connection, decoupled from interfaces + addr->conn map
description: Replace TunnelRegistry HashMap<EndpointId,String> (meshd.rs:1147, peer->ifname) with a connection manager: HashMap<EndpointId,Connection> plus reverse HashMap<Ipv4Addr(10.254.x),Connection> for demux. Unify serve_tunnel(:1153), connector_loop(:691), dialer(:632) to register CONNECTIONS not per-peer TUNs. Dial/redial in background, decoupled from any interface; a dropped conn must NOT drop an interface. At connect time compute backhaul_addr(peer) forward to populate addr->conn.
acceptance_criteria: Connection map maintained across dial/redial; addr->conn lookup returns the right conn; zero interfaces created per peer.
close_reason: Connection manager DONE + integrated (meshd.rs). ConnManager: HashMap<EndpointId,Connection> + reverse HashMap<Ipv4Addr(10.254.x),Connection>, register/deregister update both atomically (addr = backhaul_addr(peer)), one-conn-per-peer invariant. serve_overlay_conn + connector_loop_overlay + OverlayHandler register connections (no per-peer iface) and pump datagrams to the shared mjolnir0 writer. Daemon builds (--features daemon exit 0), clippy 0 warnings, and the single-node --overlay smoke (spike/buw-overlay-smoke.sh) confirms the wiring starts cleanly. Per-peer path untouched (opt-in --overlay).

title: buw spike: babel neighbour-discovery over the overlay TUN (emulate ff02::1:6 hello fan-out/inject)
description: babel discovers neighbours via IPv6 link-local multicast ff02::1:6; a TUN has no L2 multicast so hellos never cross. The daemon must EMULATE multicast: capture babel's outbound hello/IHU multicast on mjolnir0 and fan them to EVERY peer iroh connection, and inject peer-received hellos back onto mjolnir0 so babel sees neighbours. This is the trickiest correctness bit; buw design says SPIKE IT FIRST. Standalone/throwaway scaffold OK (does not need the full buw.2 TUN machinery).
design: The unicast data-plane demux (LPM by dest IP) cannot route multicast (ff02::/16 has no unicast next-hop). Special-case on read-from-TUN: if dest is link-local multicast (ff02::1:6), REPLICATE the datagram to all peer connections instead of LPM; on inbound, if the decap'd pkt is that multicast, write to mjolnir0 as-is (kernel delivers to babel on the iface). Watch: babel uses fe80:: link-local src + iface index; ensure injected pkt scope/iface is correct. FALLBACK if raw multicast injection misbehaves: babeld supports explicit UNICAST neighbours — configure each peer's overlay LL addr as a static unicast neighbour, sidestepping multicast entirely. Spike BOTH and compare.
acceptance_criteria: Two daemons, one throwaway TUN each, one real iroh conn: babel forms an adjacency over the overlay, exchanges Hello/IHU (visible under babeld -d), and installs each other's /24. No per-peer TUN involved.
notes:
SPIKE IN PROGRESS (session 2026-06-30). Built: tun/mcast.rs (multicast classifier, reusable by buw.4) + tun/overlay.rs (single-TUN N-peer fan-out multiplexer with multicast emulation) — both unit-tested (6 tests green). Plus a spike node bin (buw-overlay-spike, UDP stands in for the iroh DatagramConn seam) + spike/buw-multicast-spike.sh (2 netns, real babeld, veth+UDP underlay, tcpdump + route-table verdict). babeld installed on dev box.

FINDINGS SO FAR (empirical, real babeld 1.13 over the overlay TUN):
1. Multicast emulation WORKS at packet level — babel Hellos to ff02::1:6 are read off mjolnir0, flooded over UDP, and delivered onto the peer's mjolnir0 (confirmed via tcpdump: fe80::1 hellos appear on B's TUN). Both nodes form a Neighbour entry for each other.
2. BUG #1 FIXED: connected-UDP ICMP footgun — a startup-race ICMP port-unreachable latched ECONNREFUSED on the connected socket, killing the receiver task (asymmetric stall). Switched to unconnected send_to/recv_from. This is a property of the UDP stand-in, NOT the overlay; iroh would not have it.
3. rxcost RESOLVES correctly to 96 once hellos accumulate (reach 8000->c000->e000). Metric logic is fine.
4. REMAINING BLOCKER: packet LOSS. babeld emits ~11 hellos but only ~3-4 reach the peer TUN; txcost stays 65535 (INFINITY) because each side barely receives the other's hellos, so IHUs carry infinity -> routes installed 'unreachable'. Loss localized to the reader hop (rd-tun -> tx-peer). Instrumented with RUST_LOG=overlay=debug per-packet counters; diagnosing whether the async tun-crate reader is dropping. NOT yet PASS.
close_reason:
PROVEN (2026-06-30, real babeld 1.13 over a single overlay TUN, 2 netns, UDP standing in for the iroh DatagramConn seam). RESULT: zero packet loss, neighbour reach=ffff rxcost=96 txcost=96 cost=96 (full bidirectional adjacency), and each node's client /24 propagated across the SINGLE overlay TUN as a REACHABLE babel route (A: 10.42.2.0/24 via 10.254.0.2 dev mjolnir0; B: 10.42.1.0/24 via 10.254.0.1) — no per-peer TUN. Multicast emulation (flood ff02::1:6 to all peers, inject received onto TUN) is sufficient; the unicast-neighbour fallback was NOT needed.

DELIVERABLES (committed-quality, on branch, uncommitted): tun/mcast.rs (multicast classifier, reusable by buw.4), tun/overlay.rs (single-TUN N-peer fan-out multiplexer w/ multicast emulation), both unit-tested; src/bin/buw-overlay-spike.rs + spike/buw-multicast-spike.sh (repeatable harness). 84 lib tests pass, clippy clean.

THREE bugs found & fixed during the spike — ALL test-rig artifacts, none in the overlay design:
(1) connected-UDP ICMP footgun -> use unconnected send_to/recv_from.
(2) veth ARP EHOSTUNREACH -> not the real cause; see (3).
(3) ROOT CAUSE of the loss: babeld redistributed the peer's UNDERLAY /32 and, on the initial-loss-induced infinite metric, installed 'unreachable 10.0.0.2/32 proto babel' — MORE SPECIFIC than the on-link veth /24 — which poisoned the very transport the overlay rides on (feedback loop). Fixed by denying redistribution of the underlay + overlay-link blocks and only redistributing the claimed client /24 (exactly what the production renderer already does). Real iroh transport is immune (it does not route through the kernel FIB), but see the buw.5 note.

CONCLUSION: the buw single-TUN, multi-neighbour data plane is VIABLE. Proceed with buw.2->buw.5.

title: meshd hangs ~15s after lan_tunnels=1 tunnel forms — rapid babeld procd-restart wedge (NOT auu multipath)
description: Found during the auu retest (2026-06-30, native Cudy TR3000+M3000, binary 01da2a6, lan_tunnels=1). SEQUENCE: tunnel up kind=DIRECT paths=1 via derived addr :49737 (0yb.1 works) -> babel_reconciler restarts babeld on each input change during startup convergence (tunnel-iface added, then subnet claim arrives): 'babeld config changed — restarting babeld (procd)' at +1s,+6s,+11s -> meshd goes FULLY SILENT at the +11s restart (no logs for minutes; mj-peer iface removed; process ALIVE, 3-4 threads, S state, all wchan empty). ROOT CAUSE (strong): babeld_service('restart') = tokio::process::Command '/etc/init.d/mjolnir-babeld restart' .status().await (meshd:1032). Rapid repeated procd restart of a sibling service FROM WITHIN the procd-managed meshd (m8t) wedges — the .status().await (or procd/ubus) never returns -> reconciler loop stalls and the daemon stops functioning. Only manifests with lan_tunnels=1 because the extra per-peer tunnel interface adds the config churn; lan_tunnels=0 has only the stable br-mesh L2 (no churn, no hang). FIX DIRECTIONS: (a) debounce/coalesce the reconcile (wait for inputs to settle; don't restart babeld on every transient change); (b) timeout-guard babeld_service so a wedged restart can't stall the reconciler; (c) prefer SIGHUP/ubus reload over full init restart where possible; (d) buw (single overlay TUN) eliminates per-tunnel babel config churn entirely = the structural fix. RELATES: m8t (procd babeld supervision), buw (validated — per-peer-tunnel babel churn is real+harmful), auu (multipath ruled out). DEPLOY FIX: install-node.sh must apk add kmod-tun when tunnels may be enabled (retest first failed /dev/net/tun ENOENT).
notes: TRACE COMPLETE 2026-07-01. It is a GENUINE DEADLOCK, not a panic cascade. Added a panic-logging hook (std::panic::set_hook -> tracing::error) and rebuilt/redeployed (b9f58ad2); repro'd lan_tunnels=1 -> NO panic line on either node. Ruled OUT: babeld restart (qz9 fix works), iroh multipath (0 occurrences, paths=1 DIRECT), silent panic cascade (no panic), busy-spin (threads futex-parked not spinning), blocked I/O syscall (no thread in read/recvmsg), and the claim path's lock scoping (clean — store lock dropped before every await; assign_client_addr completes, logs EEXIST). CONFIRMED via decisive test: at +20s (before 60s idle timeout) mj-peer GONE, babel route to peer /24 GONE, tunnel /31 ping 100% loss, meshd frozen since ~+8s, ALL worker threads futex-parked (1 epoll driver). Freeze is ~right after the claim's GOSSIP PUBLISH (sync.publish -> iroh-gossip broadcast) with a per-peer tunnel up. LEADING SUSPECT: a lock/await deadlock in gossip-publish-over-iroh contending with the per-peer tunnel datagram path on the shared iroh endpoint (both freeze). NEXT PROBE if pursued directly: bisect — (a) tunnel up + skip the claim's gossip publish; (b) tunnel up + no claim — to isolate publish-vs-claim-vs-tunnel. STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION: buw (single overlay TUN) removes the per-peer mj-peer tunnels that co-occur with this deadlock and is the endgame anyway — likely dissolves qz9 without chasing an iroh-gossip/tunnel deadlock in code buw replaces. Nodes stable at lan_tunnels=0; panic hook is a keeper.

title: Cross-site design fork: one stable overlay TUN multiplexing all peers (kills per-peer mj-peer interfaces + O(N^2) interface churn)
description: Internet/cross-site mode still creates one mj-peer-<id> /31 TUN per peer (tun/iface.rs, pick_link_31), so babeld config churns as peers come/go and a full routing mesh implies O(N^2) interfaces + babeld reconfig. The O(N^2) is NOT a property of babel — babel runs fine over ONE multi-access interface with many neighbors (that is exactly LAN mode, type wired, O(N)). The fork: present a SINGLE stable overlay TUN (e.g. mjolnir0) that the daemon multiplexes by routing each packet to the right iroh connection by dest IP. Then babeld sees one interface with many neighbors, config is static even across sites, and you can keep only a bounded set of iroh connections (topological neighbors / on-demand) and let babel multi-hop over the overlay — O(N) per node, not O(N^2). Decide this before building out cross-site. Urgency/approach depends on the auu retest (whether the iroh multipath instability was real iroh-1.0 or an L23-specific network quirk): if multipath is fine, per-peer tunnels are less painful but the single-overlay TUN is still the right scaling/config answer.
design: Centerpiece data plane: the single overlay TUN that makes the encrypted cross-internet L3 overlay actually work. PROVEN PATTERN — Tailscale/Nebula/ZeroTier/tinc all use one tun + internal dest->peer demux (not a research gamble). ARCHITECTURE: (1) one overlay TUN mjolnir0, node overlay addr from 10.254.x; (2) connection manager node-id<->iroh conn (dial/redial, decoupled from interfaces); (3) FIB mirror: subscribe netlink routes on mjolnir0, build a dest-prefix->next-hop-peer LPM trie. Outbound = read pkt from mjolnir0, LPM(dest)->peer conn->one iroh datagram. Inbound = write pkt to mjolnir0; kernel forwards/local-delivers (ip_forward=1 gives multi-hop OVER the overlay). (4) babel runs on mjolnir0 as ONE multi-access interface (reuse the type-wired render). UNKNOWNS: (a) babel finds neighbors via link multicast (ff02::1:6); a TUN has no L2 multicast, so the daemon must EMULATE multicast by fanning babel hellos out to every peer connection + injecting received ones — the trickiest correctness bit. (b) iroh connection churn (auu) still exists per-connection, BUT buw makes it NON-FATAL: a dropped conn no longer drops a babel interface and collapses the config (that WAS the auu death loop) — babel just reroutes via another neighbor while the daemon re-dials in the background. So buw likely TAMES auu even if multipath churn persists. EFFORT: ~1-2wk for a solid prototype; the genuinely new code is the FIB-mirror/demux (a few hundred lines; treebitmap-style LPM + rtnetlink already in the tree) plus the multicast emulation. WALKING SKELETON: 2 nodes, single TUN each, 1 connection, babel over it, ping across the internet; then 3-4 nodes with one transit-only node to prove multi-hop-over-overlay. Connection-stability confidence depends on the auu retest. Decide full-mesh connections (simple, O(N) conns) vs neighbor-only (scalable, relies on overlay forwarding) — full-mesh is fine for the demo.
notes:
BUILD SPEC + auu RESULT FOLDED IN (2026-06-30, post lan_tunnels retest -> qz9).

UNKNOWN #2 RESOLVED FAVOURABLY: auu multipath RULED OUT natively (kind=DIRECT paths=1, no churn). iroh single-path connections are STABLE; buw's only instability source was per-peer babel-config CHURN, and qz9 is direct proof it is real+harmful (3 babeld procd-restarts in 11s wedged meshd). qz9 names buw the STRUCTURAL fix. So buw is now empirically motivated and de-risked.

TOUCH-MAP (current-tree symbols/lines):
- tun/iface.rs spawn_tunnel()+struct Tunnel (per-peer mj-peer-<id> /31 + v6 LL + spawn_encap_pair; Drop=abort->kernel drops iface) -> REPLACE with single mjolnir0 bring-up (one TUN, MTU 1300=TUNNEL_MTU, carries this node 10.254.x/16).
- tun/encap.rs spawn_encap_pair() today 1:1 TUN<->conn -> BECOMES the mux/demux: read mjolnir0 -> LPM(dest)->peer conn->datagram; recv on ANY conn -> write mjolnir0. FIB-mirror lookup + babel multicast fan-out live here.
- meshd.rs TunnelRegistry HashMap<EndpointId,String> (:1147 peer->ifacename) -> CONNECTION MANAGER HashMap<EndpointId,Connection> + reverse HashMap<Ipv4Addr 10.254.x,Connection> for demux. serve_tunnel (:1153) + connector_loop (:691) + dialer loop (:632) unify to register CONNECTIONS not per-peer TUNs.
- meshd.rs babel_reconciler (:1057) renders from TunnelRegistry -> render ONE static mjolnir0 iface always; per-peer ifaces vanish -> no config churn -> kills qz9 at the source.
- babel/config.rs render_babeld_conf -> collapse per-mj-peer 'type tunnel' lines to ONE mjolnir0 interface (reuse type-wired render).
- NEW FIB mirror: subscribe rtnetlink RTM_NEWROUTE/DELROUTE on mjolnir0, maintain dest-prefix->next-hop(10.254.x) LPM trie (treebitmap/ip_network_table; rtnetlink already a dep).
- deploy: install-node.sh must apk add kmod-tun (qz9 deploy finding: first retest hit /dev/net/tun ENOENT).

ADDRESSING + KEYING (resolved, NO hash inversion): mjolnir0 carries backhaul_addr(node_id)=10.254.<blake3> in the shared 10.254.0.0/16 (link.rs:39). All nodes on-link on that /16 -> babel treats mjolnir0 like the L2 segment it already uses. At connect time you know the peer EndpointId AND compute backhaul_addr(peer) forward -> populate addr->conn then. babel installs client-/24 via 10.254.<peer> dev mjolnir0; demux=LPM(dest)->10.254.<peer>->conn. 10.255/16 LINK_BLOCK + pick_link_31 become UNUSED.

UNIFICATION CALL (recommend U2 hybrid): mjolnir0 = ONE iface for ALL cross-site/relay peers (replaces the N per-peer tunnels); br-mesh STAYS the native same-site L2 (type wired, no QUIC encap on the local radio hop). babel runs on both = TWO static ifaces, O(1) not O(N), zero per-peer churn. Keeps the buw win + kills qz9 WITHOUT taxing same-site hops with QUIC CPU/overhead (the iroh-use-policy efficiency concern). U1 (everything over mjolnir0 incl. same-site) is a FUTURE simplification: gains true e2e encryption on every hop but taxes the local radio with encap. Do NOT do U1 first.

LOOP/EDGE: rely on kernel ip_forward + IP TTL for transit (a reconvergence loop self-terminates at TTL=0, no custom hop logic). Demux LPM-miss -> drop + rate-limited warn. The ONE genuinely new correctness bit: babel neighbour discovery uses link multicast ff02::1:6 and a TUN has no L2 multicast -> daemon must fan babel hellos out to every peer conn + inject received ones. Spike this first.

E2E NUGGET (talk): traffic over mjolnir0 is QUIC-encrypted end-to-end between daemon endpoints; 802.11s SAE only encrypts each radio hop (intermediate mesh nodes see plaintext). Under U1 even same-site hops become e2e-confidential.

RELATES: qz9 (validated buw; band-aids=reconcile debounce + timeout-guard babeld_service), auu (multipath ruled out -> unknown #2 favourable), 0yb/0yb.1 (gossip address book supplies the connection-manager peer set + derived-addr dialing), 5r0 (anti-entropy), a8o (egress default-route rides mjolnir0). Supersedes the per-peer-tunnel data plane. Walking skeleton unchanged but now de-risked (retest proves the underlying iroh conn holds).
OVERLAY METRIC (recorded, cross-ref 0yb): mjolnir0 uses babeld's built-in RTT metric (enable-timestamps + rtt-min/rtt-max/max-rtt-penalty/rtt-decay), NOT a fixed rxcost — measured RTT over the QUIC tunnel auto-deprioritises relay/degraded paths. Split: ETX (type wireless) for radio, RTT-metric (type tunnel + timestamps) for the overlay. Optionally enrich from iroh QUIC stats (direct-vs-relay flag, loss/cwnd).

title: Wire iroh discovery from static roster + derived address (0yb step 1, no gossip)
description: First increment of 0yb, exploiting that the underlay addr is derivable: backhaul_addr = blake3(node_id)[0..2] in 10.254/16 (tun/link.rs:39-50). Instead of relying on flat mDNS (MdnsAddressLookup) for peer socket addrs, feed iroh Endpoint.add_node_addr(node_id, NodeAddr{direct_addrs:[derived 10.254.x : well-known PORT]}) for every node-id in the STATIC roster (roster.rs / --roster / --peer). Any rostered peer becomes dialable without flat-L2 mDNS and WITHOUT gossip -- defers gossip-based roster propagation (the harder half of 0yb) per the get-more-working-now call. Decide the well-known iroh PORT convention here. Verify a node dials a NON-adjacent rostered peer over the babel-routed underlay. RelayMode stays Disabled (LAN).
notes: IMPLEMENTED step 1 (2026-06-30): pinned the LAN iroh bind to a well-known MESH_IROH_PORT=49737 and enriched the per-peer DIAL addr with the derived backhaul addr (10.254.x:PORT), gated to LAN, so tunnels dial peers by a FULLY-DERIVED address -- no flat-L2 mDNS. cargo check --features daemon clean; all 76 lib/bin unit tests pass. Bonus: the pinned port makes our derived dial-addr identical to the peer's mDNS-announced addr -> single path (reinforces the auu fix). REMAINING step 2: a DerivedAddressLookup (impl iroh AddressLookup: resolve(EndpointId)->10.254.x:PORT for ANY id) composed with MdnsAddressLookup, so the GOSSIP bootstrap also reaches non-adjacent peers by derived addr without first forming a tunnel. iroh 1.0 has NO add_node_addr; the AddressLookup trait (iroh address_lookup.rs:333) or the built-in memory lookup is the path. backhaul_addr is IPv4; IPv6 derivation = LibreMesh-interop (0vc).

title: babel leaks the WAN /32 (e.g. 192.168.0.114) into the mesh despite redistribute deny
description: On the 2-node mesh, the TR3000 learned '192.168.0.114 via <M3000 backhaul>' — the M3000's WAN (home-LAN) address — as a babel route. babeld.conf has 'redistribute ip <claimed/24> ... allow; redistribute deny' so the WAN /32 should not propagate. A /32 for a WAN host routed via the mesh also caused stray 'udhcpc no lease' noise. FIX: tighten the babeld redistribute filter to explicitly deny non-mesh prefixes (or only redistribute the claimed /24 + backhaul block).
notes: BUMPED to P1: not cosmetic. babel redistributing backhaul /32s means when a node's babeld restarts, peers install 'unreachable <backhaul>/32' blackholes that override the connected /16 — this BLACKHOLED 10.254.242.172 and nearly locked us out of the M3000 mid-deploy (recovered with 'ip route del unreachable' + a pinned link route). Fix: babel must only redistribute the client /24s, never the 10.254 backhaul addrs or WAN /32s.

title: Move babeld supervision to procd (meshd writes config + triggers reload, does not fork it)
description: meshd main() waits on tokio::signal::ctrl_c() only. procd sends SIGTERM on stop/restart, which does NOT trigger the ctrl_c handler, so meshd is killed (procd SIGKILL after timeout) WITHOUT running its graceful shutdown (BabelSupervisor::shutdown). The spawned babeld is orphaned. Each restart stacks another babeld; observed 5 concurrent on the TR3000, which broke babel (conflicting advertisements / stale 'unreachable metric -1' blackholes). FIX: also trap SIGTERM (tokio::signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate) and run the same shutdown path. Workaround: kill stray 'babeld -c' procs and let meshd reconciler respawn one.
design:
DECISION 2026-06-29: split babeld lifecycle from config. procd owns the PROCESS; meshd owns the CONFIG. This supersedes the original 'add a SIGTERM handler' fix — meshd should not supervise a child process at all (that is procd's job, and the chain procd->meshd->babeld is what orphans babeld on SIGKILL).

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. meshd: delete BabelSupervisor fork/kill. Keep babel_reconciler rendering /etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf from live state (claimed /24 + interface set). On config change, trigger a reload via '/etc/init.d/babeld restart' (babeld 1.13 EXITS on SIGHUP, so restart, not signal-reload; or use babeld's -g live-config socket as an optimization). meshd never fork()s babeld.
2. Drop --babeld spawning from the meshd init path; remove the supervisor module.
3. OpenWrt deploy: ENABLE the procd babeld service pointed at meshd's conf (set /etc/config/babeld option conf_file '/etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf', or the service command). NOTE: this session we DISABLED the stock babeld service because meshd ran its own — that flips: enable + start it. babeld now starts on boot, survives reboots, procd restart-on-crash.
4. Zero-interface guard (2zz): in the LOCAL 802.11s mesh br-mesh is permanent => babeld always has >=1 iface => no crash-loop, run persistently. Only dynamic --internet mode (per-peer TUNs) needs meshd to procd-STOP the service when the iface set empties and START when non-empty.
5. Verify: single babeld owned by procd; reboot a node -> both meshd + babeld return and routes re-converge (proves reboot-safe + m8t closed).

RELATED: the cleaner long-term fix for --internet mode is one stable overlay iface (multiplex peers over a single TUN) so babeld config is static even cross-site — separate design fork.
notes:
User approved (2026-06-29): hand babeld to procd for supervision. Reframed from SIGTERM-handler to procd-ownership. See design field.
IMPLEMENTED + validated fleet-wide 2026-06-29. meshd drops BabelSupervisor/fork; reconciler renders conf + drives /etc/init.d/mjolnir-babeld {enable,restart,stop}. New mjolnir-babeld procd service runs 'babeld -c /etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf' (respawn). Tests: 3x meshd restart -> still 1 babeld (old bug stacked 4+); TR3000 REBOOT -> procd autostarts both meshd+babeld, mesh self-heals. Deployed to TR3000 + M3000, both routing 0% loss. Dead code left: babel/supervisor.rs (BabelSupervisor) now unused -> remove (filed).
close_reason: Closed

title: Verify native babel installs the unique 10.254.x next-hop (auu blocker gone) + cross-node /24 reachability
description: The auu blocker (babel installing the duplicated 172.20.0.2 container placeholder as next-hop, metric 65535) was a RouterOS-container artifact. Native on OpenWrt there is no veth placeholder — confirm babel advertises/installs the unique per-node 10.254.x as the inter-node next-hop with a feasible metric, symmetric on both sides. Then prove forwarding: each node claims a /24 from 10.42.0.0/16 via the subnet-claim CRDT; ping a claimed /24 host from another node across mesh0. This closes the routing question that blocked the MikroTik path. Relates to auu, 2j6, nrh (re-publish claim).
notes:
SINGLE-NODE VALIDATED on TR3000 (2026-06-29): meshd procd service self-assigns 10.254.61.115/16 to br-mesh, iroh binds that one backhaul IP (auu fix confirmed natively — no 172.20 placeholder exists), gossip joins, babeld runs over br-mesh type wired, node claims 10.42.61.0/24. GAP (the native-adaptation work): install_client_route() installs the /24 via the hardcoded MikroTik container gateway --client-gateway default 172.20.0.1, which does not exist natively -> no real route, so babeld redistributes a /24 with nothing behind it. FIX NEEDED: on native OpenWrt, assign the claimed /24's .1 to the CLIENT bridge (br-lan) as a connected route (add --client-iface, default br-lan) + serve dnsmasq on it, instead of routing via a container gateway. This is the unlock for cross-node client traffic and the bridge to bead i8p (dnsmasq CRDT).
e4r IMPLEMENTED + validated single-node (2026-06-29): renamed --client-gateway -> --client-iface (default br-lan); claim now calls assign_client_addr() which puts <net>.1/24 on the client bridge as a CONNECTED route instead of routing via the nonexistent 172.20.0.1. On TR3000: br-lan gets 10.42.61.1/24, kernel has '10.42.61.0/24 dev br-lan scope link', babeld redistributes it. install_client_route() removed. Updated init script + uci config (option client_iface). NEXT: 2-node verify (node #2) — confirm peer installs 10.42.61.0/24 via 10.254.61.115 over br-mesh + cross-node ping. KNOWN: re-claim leaves a stale .1 addr (rare; acceptable for MVP). Code uncommitted.
VALIDATED 2-node, both directions (2026-06-29): TR3000<->M3000 over 802.11s. babel installs '10.42.X.0/24 via 10.254.X dev br-mesh onlink' with the UNIQUE backhaul next-hop (auu resolved natively). Cross-node ping of each node's client /24 gateway: 0% loss (~2-4ms). Required the firewall fix (mesh in lan zone) on both nodes. Reached far node via ssh -J over the mesh. DONE.

title: Radio: 2.4 GHz 802.11s backhaul + 5 GHz client AP (uci wireless) across the fleet
description: uci wireless on each node: 2.4 GHz radio = 802.11s mesh-point (mode mesh, shared mesh_id, SAE key, pinned channel) -> mesh0 bridged into br-mesh; 5 GHz radio = client AP -> br-lan. Same mesh_id + same SAE passphrase + same channel on ALL nodes. mesh0/br-mesh is the flat L2 the overlay self-assigns 10.254.x onto. Validate the 802.11s island forms (iw dev mesh0 station dump shows peers) and mDNS floods across it before layering meshd. Note mt76 SAE-mesh quirks — if 2.4 GHz mesh+SAE is flaky, fall back to open mesh on a quiet channel for bring-up. Supersedes the RouterOS-side radio assumptions; relates to b1d (wireless backhaul) and oim (L23 variant).
notes: RADIO TUNING (confirmed w/ Duke 2026-06-29): (1) DISABLE power-save on the backhaul mesh iface: iw dev <meshif> set power_save off (mt76 mesh+PS flaps) -- add as hotplug/init hook, persistent across wifi reload. (2) Set option distance <m> on the mesh radio for long/foliage links (fixes ACK timeout). (3) BAKE wpad-mesh-mbedtls INTO the image/install -- it is currently ONLY a comment in setup-wireless.sh; install-node.sh installs babeld+kmod-tun only, so flashed nodes get stock wpad-basic-mbedtls (no 802.11s SAE). Needs the swap -wpad-basic-mbedtls +wpad-mesh-mbedtls (they conflict / both provide wpad). (4) htmode A/B test -> separate bead. (5) SINGLE-CHANNEL reaffirmed: every backhaul peer shares band+channel+width+mesh_id+encryption, chosen fleet-wide ahead of time; 2.4 ch6 is non-DFS = good (no radar-induced mesh partition).

title: Flash OpenWrt 24.10.x on the 3 Cudy MT7981 boxes (revision-verify first)
description: Per-device flash. PRE-FLIGHT BRICK TRAPS: (a) WR3000 must be v1 (MT7981); v2 is a different unsupported SoC and bricks — verify via bottom label/box + stock SoC string. (b) M3000: read stock System Log for 2.5G PHY — rtl8221b -> firmware-selector id cudy_m3000-v1; yt8821 -> cudy_m3000-v2-yt8821 (needs 25.12+, NOT 24.10). (c) TR3000: pick image by flash size — cudy_tr3000-v1 (128MB) vs cudy_tr3000-256mb-v1 (256MB). (d) ANY unit serial >=2543/2544 (Nov 2025+) ships new ESMT F50L1G41LC NAND and BRICKS on OpenWrt <24.10.5 — use >=24.10.5. Procedure (Cudy two-step): stock UI 192.168.10.1 -> flash Cudy signed intermediate/unlock .bin -> reboot to temp OpenWrt LuCI 192.168.1.1 -> flash official sysupgrade, UNCHECK keep-settings, Ethernet only. Recovery: U-Boot TFTP (hold reset on power-on, TFTP server 192.168.1.1); UART 3.3V 115200 8N1 + mtk_uartboot for hard brick. Post: set root pw, opkg update, set WAN on TR3000 (gateway).
notes: TR3000 v1 FLASHED OpenWrt 25.12.4 (r32933). ESMT SPI-NAND 128 MiB (cudy_tr3000-v1 image). SN TR30002509 = pre-new-NAND batch. board cudy,tr3000-v1. radio0=2.4G(18000000.wifi), radio1=5G. eth0=2.5G WAN, eth1=1G LAN, br-lan=192.168.1.1.

title: [epic] Deploy mjolnir-mesh on Cudy MT7981 fleet (OpenWrt native, connectivity-first)
description: Bring the mesh up on fully-open hardware we control end-to-end: Cudy WR3000 v1, TR3000, M3000 — all MT7981B Filogic 820 + MT7976, one mediatek/filogic OpenWrt target, mt76 802.11s. Decisions (2026-06-28): (1) MILESTONE = connectivity-first — flash + 802.11s backhaul + meshd/babel routing client /24s cross-node using stock per-router dnsmasq; defer the dnsmasq CRDT sync to a follow-up. (2) GATEWAY/egress = TR3000 (2.5G WAN + USB3 + most RAM). (3) BACKHAUL = 2.4 GHz 802.11s (range/foliage) / 5 GHz client AP. Drops ALL RouterOS-container compromises (no container, no veth 172.20.x placeholder, no client-routing.rsc, no meshctl SSH/script-gen). KEY WIN: the auu duplicate-IP next-hop blocker is a container artifact and evaporates natively — babel runs directly on the mesh iface with the unique 10.254.x. IP scheme stays IPv4; client IPs come from CRDT-coordinated dnsmasq DHCP (not hashed); only the per-router 10.254.x underlay addr is hash-derived (fine at fleet scale). Pin OpenWrt 24.10.x (>=24.10.5) — 802.11s-LuCI known-good + new-NAND-safe. L23/MikroTik OpenWrt port (qzq et al.) stays a separate track.
notes:
PROGRESS 2026-06-29: TR3000 (node #1) fully up on OpenWrt 25.12.4 — binary runs, 802.11s mesh+AP up, meshd+babeld+CRDT all running, claimed 10.42.61.0/24, node id 06318b42...; reachable out-of-band via build-machine enp4s0<->TR3000 eth1 (192.168.1.1) and online via WAN (192.168.0.174). Remaining for first real mesh: (a) flash node #2 (WR3000 v1 / M3000 PHY check), (b) native client-subnet wiring (e4r) so the claimed /24 routes real client traffic.
MILESTONE 2026-06-29: 2-node 802.11s mesh LIVE on Cudy TR3000+M3000 (OpenWrt 25.12.4, DE). babel routes both client /24s bidirectionally with unique next-hops. Key fixes this session: e4r (native client-subnet wiring), firewall (mesh->lan zone), 25.x 802.11s via uci. Pending: dnsmasq serving the /24 (real client test, bkv), 3rd node WR3000, gossip-CRDT convergence check, optional br-client split.
CONNECTIVITY-FIRST COMPLETE 2026-06-29: real client device routes across the 2-node 802.11s mesh to the far node's LAN (bkv closed). Bugs filed: mjolnir-mesh-m8t (babeld orphan), mjolnir-mesh-ctr (WAN /32 leak). Productionize: mjolnir-mesh-bvw. Remaining: 3rd node WR3000, internet egress, push.

title: meshd: direct iroh tunnel drops ~36s in (MultipathNotNegotiated) and does not recover
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo/4pk, .181/.113, 2026-06-24). The 4pk DIRECT path forms (kind=DIRECT 10.254.x rtt~13ms) and works for ~36s, then the iroh connection/path closes with 'noq_proto::connection: failed closing path err=MultipathNotNegotiated' (also seen earlier as 'tunnel closed reason=TimedOut'). serve_tunnel drops the per-peer TUN on conn.closed() -> the mj-peer interface disappears -> babeld config goes empty -> babeld 'Eek no interfaces'. The connector_loop redials but the tunnel does not stay up (death loop persists minutes). So the direct path is not STABLE. MultipathNotNegotiated is an iroh 1.0 multipath/path-migration error — likely iroh tries to migrate/close the relay path after the direct path forms and multipath isn't negotiated, dropping the connection. INVESTIGATE: iroh 1.0 multipath config / path migration behavior; whether to enable multipath, pin the direct path, or hold the connection across path changes; idle-timeout/keepalive on the QUIC connection; whether the LAN-only (no relay) endpoint mishandles the single-path case. This is the real blocker for stable cross-mesh routing now that the backhaul/direct-path forms. Lower-layer than babeld (2zz).
notes:
RETEST RESULT 2026-06-30 (native Cudy TR3000+M3000, binary 01da2a6, lan_tunnels=1): the MultipathNotNegotiated churn did NOT recur. The tunnel formed kind=DIRECT, paths=1, dialed at the DERIVED addr 10.254.x:49737 (0yb.1 confirmed working), rtt ~5-7ms. ZERO MultipathNotNegotiated / tunnel-closed / TimedOut on BOTH sides. => the original ~36s multipath death was the RouterOS-container / L23 / duplicate-IP artifact, NOT a native iroh-1.0 problem. The multipath concern is DE-PRIORITIZED. HOWEVER a DIFFERENT blocker surfaced: meshd hangs ~10-15s after the lan_tunnels=1 tunnel forms (rapid babeld procd-restart wedge) -> filed as a new bead. Also: the retest first failed with /dev/net/tun ENOENT because kmod-tun was never installed (install-node.sh best-effort-skips it) -> installed on both nodes. Nodes recovered to lan_tunnels=0 + restarted on the 01da2a6 binary (stable; mesh data path verified M3000->TR3000 10.42.61.1 OK). Bottom line: auu's specific multipath bug appears RESOLVED natively; the path is DIRECT+single-path. Cannot yet confirm 'survives past 36s' because the reconciler hang tears it down first — that is the next thing to fix.
RETEST CONCLUSION (2026-06-30): MULTIPATH RULED OUT. Native Cudy TR3000+M3000 (binary 01da2a6, lan_tunnels=1): the per-peer iroh tunnel formed kind=DIRECT paths=1 over the derived 10.254.x addr (single pinned port) and did NOT show MultipathNotNegotiated or the ~36s death. So the original auu churn was the L23/RouterOS-container artifact (duplicate 172.20.0.2 across the shared L2 + relay-path migration) — BOTH absent on native OpenWrt — not a real iroh 1.0 multipath bug. The native single-path DIRECT connection is STABLE. The remaining instability is NOT auu: it is qz9 (meshd wedges ~15s in from rapid babeld procd-restart churn that the per-peer tunnel interface coming/going drives) — a config-churn/procd problem, fixed structurally by buw (single overlay TUN = zero per-tunnel churn) and band-aided by reconcile debounce + timeout-guarding babeld_service. RECOMMEND: close/downgrade auu once qz9 lands; re-point the apo validation that blocks on auu at qz9/buw. Implication for buw: unknown #2 (iroh connection churn) resolved FAVOURABLY — the connections are fine; the work is purely the per-interface -> single-overlay refactor.
close_reason: Multipath ruled OUT by native-hardware retest 2026-06-30: per-peer iroh tunnel formed kind=DIRECT paths=1 over the derived addr and was stable (no MultipathNotNegotiated, no ~36s death). Original churn was an L23/RouterOS-container artifact (dup 172.20.0.2 on shared L2 + relay migration), absent on native OpenWrt. Remaining instability is qz9 (gossip/tunnel deadlock), structurally dissolved by buw. No real iroh-1.0 multipath defect to fix.

title: meshd: babeld dies on SIGHUP reload and is never restarted
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo/4pk, routers .181/.113): with the 4pk DIRECT backhaul path up, the per-peer TUN tunnel forms kind=DIRECT rtt~13ms, but cross-mesh routing still fails because the ACCEPTOR's babeld dies. Sequence on .113: 'spawned babeld pid=6' -> 'babeld started' -> ~5s later 'babeld config changed — reloaded via SIGHUP' -> babeld goes SILENT forever (with -d 1 it logs constantly, so it exited). .181's babeld stays alive but its neighbour 'reach' decays to ~0 (rxcost 65535) because it stops hearing .113's hellos. The reconciler uses bare BabelSupervisor::spawn (NOT babel::run_with_restart), so a dead babeld is never revived. LEAD: an earlier run logged 'creat(/var/run/babeld.pid): File exists' — babeld's pidfile (-I default /var/run/babeld.pid) likely conflicts across reload/respawn. FIX DIRECTIONS: (1) wire babel::run_with_restart so babeld is respawned on death (it exists, unused); (2) stop babeld dying on SIGHUP — give it a clean/unique pidfile (-I) or clear it before (re)start, or verify SIGHUP-reload works in babeld 1.13 (maybe restart instead of SIGHUP); (3) reduce SIGHUP churn (only on meaningful config change). This is the last blocker for cross-mesh routing now that the direct path works.
notes: FIXED by m8t (commit cebcdcd), code-verified this session. Root cause was the reconciler using bare BabelSupervisor::spawn (dead babeld never revived) + SIGHUP killing babeld 1.13 + pidfile churn. Current babel_reconciler (mjolnir-meshd.rs ~1002-1085) drives babeld purely via procd: renders babeld.conf and on actual change calls babeld_service('restart') — a CLEAN restart, never SIGHUP — while procd (mjolnir-babeld, 'respawn 3600 5 0') independently revives babeld on crash. All three fix directions implemented; restart fires only when the rendered config changes (no churn). z1s removed the last SIGHUP code (supervisor.rs), so no SIGHUP path remains in the tree. End-to-end hardware re-validation rides on apo — reopen if the symptom recurs on the fleet.
close_reason: Closed

title: Build the overlay for OpenWrt mt76 nodes (aarch64-musl, native, + babeld pkg)
description: New mesh nodes are OpenWrt mt76 boards (MT7981/MT7986, aarch64 Cortex-A53) running the iroh+babeld overlay NATIVELY (not in a RouterOS container). Today we only cross-build armv7-musl for the MikroTik container (deploy/mikrotik). Add an aarch64-unknown-linux-musl build of mjolnir-meshd, wire up babeld (in OpenWrt's repo), and a small install/run recipe for OpenWrt (no container needed -- static musl binary runs directly). This is what makes 'runs on OpenWrt nodes' real and is the precursor to standing up the 802.11s island (b1d).
close_reason: aarch64-musl build script + native install recipe done; static binary validated on arm64 Linux (LAN mode, 10.254 backhaul self-assigned + surfaced by iroh). babeld via opkg.

title: Multi-hop discovery: babeld/mDNS synchronization (gossip-based address book)
description: Everything validated so far assumes ONE shared L2 segment, where mDNS floods to all nodes so everyone discovers everyone directly. A spread-out forest has no such segment: flat mDNS only reaches direct radio-neighbors. babeld routes TRAFFIC multi-hop fine, but a node learning how to ADDRESS a non-neighbor (the address iroh needs to dial) is unsolved by mDNS alone. Direction: stop relying on flat mDNS for mesh-wide discovery; use the roster + propagate peer addresses (node id -> reachable address) over the CRDT gossip overlay (which rides the babeld-routed IP network), the same way subnet claims propagate. mDNS stays for direct-neighbor bootstrap; gossip becomes an eventually-consistent mesh-wide address book. This synchronization between link-local discovery (mDNS) and the routed overlay (babeld+gossip) is the protocol to design+build. Platform-independent. See docs/network-coordination/radio-backhaul-and-discovery.md.
notes:
CODE-GROUNDED PROTOCOL SKETCH (2026-06-29, from architecture map). KEY ENABLER confirmed in code: the backhaul underlay addr is DERIVABLE from the node id — backhaul_addr() tun/link.rs:39-50 sets host = blake3(node_id)[0..2] in 10.254.0.0/16. So 'address book' collapses to 'roster of node-ids' + a well-known port: any node computes peer 10.254.x from peer node-id; babel already provides reachability; iroh dials it. We do NOT need to gossip (node_id -> socket-addr); we need to gossip WHICH node-ids exist. PROTOCOL: (1) mDNS = direct-neighbor bootstrap only (iroh MdnsAddressLookup already does this on the flat island; on mesh_fwding=0 it only reaches radio neighbors — that's fine, it just seeds). (2) Roster propagation over the EXISTING iroh-gossip CRDT (one topic blake3('mjolnir/mesh/crdt/v0'), meshd:355; same channel subnet claims already use) -> eventually-consistent mesh-wide set of node-ids. (3) iroh dial path change: feed Endpoint.add_node_addr(node_id, NodeAddr{ direct_addrs:[blake3(node_id)->10.254.x : PORT] }) from the roster instead of relying on flat mDNS. Relay stays Disabled (LAN). OPEN ITEMS: well-known iroh PORT convention; 10.254.x /16 host collision policy at fleet scale (currently 2 bytes of blake3); roster churn TTL/tombstone; whether iroh same-site DATA tunnels get re-enabled (gated on auu path-churn fix) or we keep plain-L2 data + iroh-for-control. DEPENDS conceptually on: babel moving from one 'type wired' br-mesh to per-radio-link peering (config.rs:77-79 assumes flat L2), and the unifying iroh-gossip<->CRDT<->babel glue that tests/two_site_netns.rs:8-11 says does not yet exist.
SAME-SITE ROUTING DECISION (2026-06-30, from the buw unification discussion). DEFAULT (keep + build first): br-mesh bridge + type wired + mesh_fwding=1 — flat 802.11s HWMP island, seamless L2 roaming, validated + fast; the expedient demo path. END-STATE for spread-out fleets (this bead): un-bridge -> run babel on the RAW mesh vif, mesh_fwding=0, type WIRELESS so babel runs ETX (per-neighbour Hello-loss link quality) and routes around weak radio links — the real reason to run babel on radio. It is a PACKAGE: raw-vif + mesh_fwding=0 + type wireless together; the keyword alone does nothing while HWMP hides the radio hops behind the bridge. TRADE-OFF: mesh_fwding=0 turns intra-island roaming from a seamless L2 event into an L3 event needing the deferred /32-host-route roaming. Same axis as the per-node /24 structure: pushing L3 to the edge buys quality routing + broadcast containment + loop-safety, priced in L2-roaming convenience. OVERLAY METRIC (refines buw, per duke): mjolnir0 should NOT use a fixed rxcost — use babeld's built-in RTT metric (enable-timestamps true + rtt-min/rtt-max/max-rtt-penalty/rtt-decay on the tunnel iface). babel timestamps its Hellos, measures end-to-end RTT over the QUIC tunnel, and penalises the metric, so a relay path (high RTT) or a degraded/lossy path (retransmit-inflated RTT) is auto-deprioritised WITHOUT hand-plumbing QUIC internals. So: ETX (type wireless) for radio, RTT-metric (type tunnel + timestamps) for the overlay. Optionally enrich by nudging the metric from iroh QUIC stats (direct-vs-relay, loss/cwnd). config.rs:77-81 renders type wired/tunnel today — add a wireless render + the rtt options.

title: Validate 4-node mesh on the wired switch (real babeld + client data path)
description: Bring up all 4 nodes on the shared wired switch with the new LAN-mode/IPv4-backhaul build. Apply container-net-lan.rsc ($meshLink = switch-facing port) on each, deploy the fresh image, run 'mesh' (defaults --lan) with a roster of all 4 ids, REAL babeld (not stubbed). Validate: (a) each node assigns its 10.254 backhaul; (b) all 6 pairs form kind=DIRECT tunnels (path logger); (c) CRDT converges, 4 distinct /24 claims; (d) babeld propagates all /24s; (e) a client on node A's LAN reaches a host on node B's LAN across the mesh. This is the hardware proof of the 4pk mechanism before the wireless backhaul swap. Blocks the wireless-backhaul work (prove software on the wire first).

title: LAN-mode backhaul: container on shared L2 + IPv6 link-local node discovery
description: Pivot the same-site mesh off the relay/double-NAT path. (1) Put the meshd container on the shared L2 segment (bridge veth-mesh to the switch-facing port) so peers are mutually reachable instead of buried behind each node's masquerade. (2) Default the daemon to --lan (mDNS + relays disabled, fully offline). (3) Node-to-node backhaul addressing via IPv6 link-local (zero-config, collision-free, already proven for babeld in op4), with a node-id-derived IPv4 fallback if iroh mDNS can't dial link-local. Unifies the address space so all 4 same-room nodes form DIRECT iroh paths; sidesteps mjolnir-mesh-67h (no relay exists offline). CRDT keeps overlay/client /24 addressing unchanged.
notes: DIRECT PATH HARDWARE-VALIDATED 2026-06-24 (routers .181/.113). Containers bridged onto the shared mgmt LAN (no dedicated backhaul port on this bench, so veth-mesh added to the default bridge — additive, mgmt/SSH untouched). meshd self-assigned 10.254.x (.181=10.254.23.7, .113=10.254.3.43) and the per-peer tunnel formed kind=DIRECT remote=10.254.x rtt~13ms, NO RELAY. KEY FIX (committed c592ecc): the RouterOS container names the NIC 'veth-mesh', NOT eth0 — backhaul iface discovery is now name-agnostic via /sys/class/net (sole non-loopback fallback + startup-race retry). REMAINING 4pk hardware items: babeld routing of client /24s is blocked by the new babeld-dies-on-SIGHUP bug (see mjolnir-mesh-2zz); 4-node (not 2) on the switch still to do.

title: meshd: TUN needs an IPv6 link-local for babeld to form an adjacency
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo, routers .181/.113): babeld parses its config and runs, but installs NO cross-mesh route — the per-peer mj-peer-X TUN is created with only an IPv4 /31, and Babel runs its protocol over IPv6 link-local (fe80::), exchanging both v4 and v6 routes over it. With no IPv6 LL on the TUN, babeld can't send/receive Hellos -> no neighbour -> no routes. Symptom: ping A->B (10.42.23.1 -> 10.42.3.1) returns 'TTL exceeded' from the container (172.20.0.2) — the packet reaches container A but has no tunnel route for B's /24, so it loops container<->router until TTL dies. (This also proves the RouterOS->container->forward data path is CORRECT; only babeld's route is missing.) FIX: in tun/iface.rs spawn_tunnel, ensure the TUN has an IPv6 link-local — either enable IPv6 on the iface (net.ipv6.conf.<if>.disable_ipv6=0 + addr_gen_mode) or explicitly assign a deterministic fe80:: address per side via rtnetlink (like the /31 v4 assign). Also bump babeld verbosity (-d 1/2) in the supervisor so neighbour/route activity is visible (babeld is silent at default level).
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. IPv6 link-local on the TUN (fe80::<host-octet>) lets babeld form a Babel adjacency: .181 logged 'Neighbour fe80::d5 dev mj-peer-9b8c14e' and installed '10.42.3.0/24 ... via mj-peer ... (installed)'. Also dropped babeld -D (was daemonising/detaching it) and added -d 1 for visibility. babeld now routes; the residual ping failure is asymmetric data-plane delivery (67h), not op4.

title: meshd: install local /24 back-route on claim (so babeld can redistribute it)
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2): babeld starts with ifaces=1 but has nothing to redistribute — the container has no route for its OWN claimed /24, so 'redistribute ip 10.42.x.0/24' matches nothing and no cross-mesh route propagates. This is the 'per-router /24 -> local LAN' caveat from client-routing.md. Fix: when meshd claims /24 X, also install a kernel route 'X via <container-gateway 172.20.0.1> dev eth0' in the container netns (rtnetlink, like the TUN setup). That (a) gives babeld a concrete route to redistribute and (b) sends inbound mesh traffic for X back to the router for local-client delivery. Needs the container gateway (derive from default route, or a --client-gateway flag).
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. meshd installs '<claimed /24> via 172.20.0.1' on claim (--client-gateway, default 172.20.0.1). Both routers logged 'installed client subnet route subnet=10.42.x.0/24 gateway=172.20.0.1'; babeld then redistributes the /24 (peer's babeld learned + installed it). Confirmed on hardware.

title: meshd: babeld stdio should inherit, not pipe (deadlock + visibility)
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2, .181/.113): BabelSupervisor::spawn sets .stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()) but nothing drains them. babeld -D logs to stdout; once the ~64KB pipe buffer fills, babeld BLOCKS on write and hangs (routing silently dies). Also we get zero babeld visibility — can't see neighbor/route exchange in the container log. Fix: use Stdio::inherit() so babeld's output flows to the container log (RouterOS captures it), or actively drain the pipes. Inherit also gives us the route-exchange diagnostics we currently lack.
close_reason: FIXED + hardware-validated. babeld stdio now inherited (was piped+undrained); babeld's own logs reach the container log — which is how the config parse error AND the txcost/asymmetry were diagnosed. Also dropped -D so babeld stays a foreground child (valid PID, working SIGHUP).

title: meshd: multi-peer tunnel manager (N concurrent /31 tunnels)
description: Today the daemon runs exactly one tunnel (one tun-listen OR one tun-connect). Replace with a manager that maintains a peer->Tunnel map: dial/accept every peer in the roster, bring up a per-peer /31 TUN via spawn_tunnel, redial on drop, tear down on removal, and expose the live set of peer-interface names (needed by babeld). Foundation for routing — babel and forwarding both key off the live tunnel set.
notes: IMPLEMENTED + smoke-tested (off-hardware). New 'mesh --roster <file>' subcommand runs the full multi-peer daemon: builds endpoint, loads PeerRoster (kui), accepts inbound TUN_ALPN tunnels AND dials peers. Design: (1) ONE tunnel per peer-pair via node-id tie-break — the lexicographically-lower id dials, the higher accepts (mirrors pick_link_31's ordering) so both ends never race to create the same deterministic /31 'mj-peer-<id>' interface. (2) Shared TunnelRegistry (Arc<Mutex<HashMap<EndpointId,String>>>) maps peer->iface, enforces one-tunnel-per-peer atomically (sentinel reserve), and is the seam babeld (83k) will read for the live tunnel set. (3) serve_tunnel() shared by accept + dial paths. (4) connector_loop per dial-peer: dial -> serve -> redial with capped (1s..30s) exponential backoff, resets on success. Local smoke test (self ac9c.. < two peer ids) confirmed: roster loaded, both dialed, 'mesh up dialing=2', redial fired after dial-fail. 136 tests pass, clippy clean. NOT YET hardware-validated: real TUN-per-peer bring-up + concurrent multi-peer is Linux+root — rides on bead apo (e2e). FOLLOW-UPS (noted, not blocking): roster is loaded once (no dynamic SIGHUP reload yet); registry not yet exposed via a public getter (83k adds that); bare-id roster entries need relay/discovery to resolve addrs (use full address blobs in relay-less mode).
close_reason: Multi-peer tunnel manager implemented + locally smoke-tested (roster load, id tie-break, per-peer redial/backoff, one-tunnel-per-peer registry). Builds on hardware-validated spawn_tunnel. Concurrent multi-peer hardware e2e tracked in mjolnir-mesh-apo.

title: meshd: peer roster / membership source (which peers to dial)
description: The daemon currently learns a single peer from a pasted address blob (tun-connect <addr>). For a real mesh it needs a set of peers to maintain connections to. MVP: a static roster file (node addresses) the daemon reads and dials/accepts; later replaced/augmented by CRDT membership. Output: a resolved, updatable peer set consumed by the multi-peer tunnel manager.
notes: LIB INCREMENT LANDED (ultrawork): src/roster.rs — PeerRoster/PeerEntry/RosterError, load()/load_from_str(), file format (token + optional label, # comments, blank lines, first-wins dedup, empty-ok). 8 unit tests, lib stays iroh-free (tokens stored verbatim; daemon parses). REMAINING: daemon CLI flag (e.g. --roster <file>) + actually dialing/accepting the peer set — that consumption is part of mjolnir-mesh-23a (multi-peer manager), which depends on this.
close_reason: Peer roster membership source complete: src/roster.rs (PeerRoster, 8 tests) landed AND now consumed by the daemon — 'mesh --roster <file>' (23a) loads it and dials the peer set. Daemon-reads-and-dials requirement satisfied.

title: Data plane: carry client LAN traffic across the mesh (tr6 P2+P3)
description: Today mjolnir-meshd is the P1 connectivity proof: it brings up ONE per-peer /31 TUN (tun-listen/tun-connect) and UDP-probes between the two link addresses. It does NOT yet carry a wifi/LAN client's traffic across the mesh — the binary references none of the babel/alloc/crdt modules, which exist only as libraries. This epic wires the 'top layer' so a client on router A can reach a host on router B's LAN: multi-peer tunnels + subnet claim (alloc/CRDT) + babeld routing + container IP forwarding + RouterOS route glue. Groups tr6's P2 (multi-peer + subnet alloc + CRDT gossip) and P3 (babeld cross-site routing). Done when the e2e client-traffic test passes on hardware.

title: meshd tunnel data-plane reliability: only 1/5 probes cross
description: On the first live two-router mesh (meshctl deploy --all, routers .181/.113), the tun-connect tunnel established and IP traffic crossed (DATA PLANE WORKS) but only 1/5 UDP probes got replies — 4 timed out. Investigate: relay-only path (no hole-punch yet?) loss/latency, MTU on the /31 TUN over iroh datagrams, probe timing vs tunnel warmup, or RouterOS container UDP handling. Reproduce via the connector container log. Not a blocker for connectivity but needed for a usable mesh.
notes: ADDRESSED by 4pk's direct path (2026-06-24). Root cause was relay-path loss/asymmetry; with the direct LAN backhaul the per-peer tunnel is kind=DIRECT rtt~13ms (no relay), so the data plane is reliable. Not formally re-measuring the old 1/5 probe yet because the routing test is blocked by the separate babeld-dies-on-SIGHUP bug (mjolnir-mesh-2zz), but the relay-asymmetry that caused 67h is structurally gone. Close once a clean client-traffic test passes on the direct path.
close_reason: Relay-asymmetry root cause structurally gone via 4pk direct path (per-peer tunnel kind=DIRECT rtt~13ms, no relay). Validation blocker 2zz (babeld dies on SIGHUP) is now CLOSED (2026-06-29). Final client-traffic validation on the direct path is tracked under apo. Defect resolved.

title: P1.5: --lan mode — mDNS LAN discovery (no relay/internet)
description: Added --lan: Endpoint::empty_builder(RelayMode::Disabled) + MdnsAddressLookup (iroh address-lookup-mdns feature). No pkarr/n0-DNS (no internet dependency, no spam), relays off, mDNS peer discovery on the LAN. tun-connect/connect accept a bare 64-hex node id (parse_peer), resolved via mDNS. Validated locally on macOS: connect-by-node-id → mDNS resolves → connection established, no relay. Unblocks same-switch swarm testing without fighting container internet egress / canary relays. REQUIRES containers on the same L2 LAN (bridged, not NAT'd) for mDNS multicast + direct reachability.

title: P1 probe: TUN-in-RouterOS-container (mjolnir-meshd tun-test)
description: Gating check for the L3 data plane on MikroTik: does a TUN device create inside a RouterOS container (needs /dev/net/tun + CAP_NET_ADMIN)? Added 'tun-test' subcommand that creates a throwaway /31 via PeerInterface::create and tears it down. If it works, full P1 (IrohDatagramConn + spawn_encap_pair + connect-and-tunnel) is viable on MikroTik; if not, rethink packet path (userspace, etc.). Test on the 4-router LAN testbed. Also fixed latent breakage: tun/iface.rs never compiled for linux target (missing futures-util dep + tun::Error conversion) — now cross-compiles.
notes: ANSWERED YES (2026-06-23): TUN-in-RouterOS-container WORKS. During meshctl deploy --all on .113, the tun-connect container brought up a TUN device (iface mj-peer-81f543c, /31 10.255.53.213<->.212) and a UDP probe crossed it: 'tunnel ping 1: reply from 10.255.53.212 in 168ms — DATA PLANE WORKS'. So /dev/net/tun + CAP_NET_ADMIN are available in the RouterOS container. Note: only 1/5 probes replied (reliability follow-up filed).
close_reason: TUN-in-container confirmed viable on hardware: tunnel established + IP packet crossed (DATA PLANE WORKS)

title: P0: iroh connectivity MVP (mjolnir-meshd id/listen/connect)
description: First slice of the headless daemon (tr6). Minimal [[bin]] mjolnir-meshd in crates/mjolnir-mesh behind a 'daemon' feature (keeps lib + tests iroh-free). Subcommands: id (print EndpointId + addr blob), listen (accept on mjolnir/mesh/v0 ALPN, echo ping datagrams), connect <addr> (dial, round-trip a datagram, report RTT). Persisted identity via --secret-file (gen on first run) / IROH_SECRET. NO TUN — de-risks iroh-in-container before the TUN-in-container question. Reuses iroh 0.96 patterns from mjolnir-node.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --features daemon succeeds; two local instances complete a connect round-trip; lib tests still build without the daemon feature.
close_reason: Done: mjolnir-meshd builds with --features daemon; loopback round-trip verified (2.8ms, --no-relay --bind 127.0.0.1:0); lib stays iroh-free without the feature (65 lib tests pass). Deploy Dockerfile/build.sh updated to pass --features daemon.

title: Headless iroh-transport router daemon (mjolnir-meshd)
description: The only [[bin]] today is 'mjolnir-mesh' built from crates/mjolnir-node — the AUDIO example client (pulls mjolnir-audio -> cpal/ALSA + tract-onnx). Wrong thing to deploy on a headless router: needs audio HW, heavy, hard to static-cross-compile. Need a headless daemon binary for the substrate (crates/mjolnir-mesh: alloc/babel/crdt/tun). OPEN DESIGN QUESTION: substrate-only, or also include the iroh QUIC transport (currently only in mjolnir-node/mjolnir-moq, not in mjolnir-mesh deps)? This binary is the prerequisite for any router container deploy.
design: SCOPE RESOLVED: substrate + iroh transport, iroh-first, NO audio. Router = transport-only ('shuttle packets'). Substrate (crates/mjolnir-mesh) is transport-agnostic via DatagramConn trait seam; reuse iroh wiring pattern from mjolnir-node/mesh.rs (Endpoint + IROH_SECRET + MemoryLookup + ticket bootstrap), drop audio/room. MISSING GLUE: (1) production DatagramConn impl over iroh::Connection (only test MockConn exists); (2) PeerInterface::create drops TUN device handle (let _device) - must expose read/write halves for spawn_encap_pair. PHASES: P0 iroh endpoint + QUIC connectivity between two nodes (NO TUN - de-risks iroh-in-container before TUN-in-container risk). P1 IrohDatagramConn + single TUN /31 link via spawn_encap_pair (ping across mesh). P2 multi-peer full mesh + subnet alloc + CRDT gossip. P3 babeld supervision for cross-site routing. MTU: keep TUN MTU ~1300 (under QUIC datagram limit, else EncapError::DatagramTooLarge).
notes: Scope decided 2026-06-18: audio is a client-layer concern (mjolnir-node), router only carries packets. See memory project_router_role_decision.

title: US-002: Subnet claim FWW merge + conflict resolution
description:
Implement first-writer-wins merge for `/subnets/{cidr}` entries. Mirrors the existing LeaseEntry FWW logic but keyed by CIDR rather than IP.

## Behavior
- On receiving a `SubnetClaimUpdate`, compare incoming `claimed_at` HLC against any local entry for the same CIDR.
- If incoming HLC is strictly lower → incoming wins; replace local entry; emit a conflict event so the losing local owner can rewrite its claim and reconfigure babeld.
- If incoming HLC is strictly higher → discard.
- If equal → tie-break on `owner_node_id` lexicographic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `resolve_subnet_conflict(&a, &b)` returns (winner, loser) deterministically
- [ ] CRDT store `put_subnet` returns `MergeResult::Inserted | Updated | Unchanged | Conflict { winner, loser }`
- [ ] Unit tests cover: same CIDR + different owners + different HLCs (both orderings), equal HLC tie-break, identical entries (Unchanged)
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-003: Subnet allocation — derive preferred /24, check CRDT, claim
description:
On first boot at a new site (no local peers detected within the 10s detection window), the daemon must pick a /24 from the mesh address space (default `10.42.0.0/16`) and claim it.

## Algorithm
1. Enumerate current `/subnets/{cidr}` entries from the CRDT.
2. Preferred /24 = `10.42.{ blake3(node_id) % 256 }.0/24`.
3. If preferred is already claimed, increment third octet until a free one is found.
4. Wait 10s claim cooldown; if a local peer appears in that window, abort and join Mode 1.
5. Write `/subnets/{cidr}` with current HLC and gossip.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `pick_subnet(node_id, claimed: &HashSet<IpNet>, base: IpNet)` returns deterministic preferred /24 + fallback walk
- [ ] Integration test with a mocked CRDT store: 3 routers, distinct node_ids, all pick non-overlapping /24s
- [ ] Cooldown path: if a `local_peer_detected` signal fires within 10s, no claim is written
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-001: CRDT data model — SubnetClaim, IpAddr-typed leases, gossip enum update
description:
Replace the placeholder routing types in the CRDT data model with the subnet-claim ledger and make IP fields version-agnostic.

## Changes
- Add `SubnetClaim { cidr: IpNet, owner_node_id: String, site_name: Option<String>, claimed_at: HLC }` (introduce `ipnet` crate dep).
- Remove any `RouteEntry` / `RouteUpdate` if present in code (none currently — greenfield).
- Change `LeaseEntry.ip: Ipv4Addr` → `LeaseEntry.ip: std::net::IpAddr`. Same for `DnsEntry.ip` and `ServiceEntry.ip`.
- Update `GossipMessage` enum: drop `RouteUpdate`; add `SubnetClaimUpdate { cidr: String, entry: SubnetClaim }` and `SubnetClaimRelease { cidr: String, hlc: HLC }`.
- Postcard round-trip tests for all updated/new types.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SubnetClaim` struct defined with the four fields above
- [ ] `LeaseEntry.ip`, `DnsEntry.ip`, `ServiceEntry.ip` are all `IpAddr`
- [ ] `GossipMessage` enum reflects the new variants (no `RouteUpdate`)
- [ ] Postcard serialize/deserialize round-trips covered by unit tests for each type
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: pkg-cache push must carry dependency CLOSURES, not single packages (kmod deps unresolvable offline)
description: Found updating the M3000 (no feeds) 2026-07-01: install-node.sh's cache-push only pushes the packages named in $MISSING, but 'apk add --no-network <file>' cannot resolve dependencies it doesn't have as local files — kmod-rt2800-usb failed with 'required by: world[...]' even though the ENTIRE rt2800 chain sat in pkg-cache (the applier warned and continued, apply still OK). Worked around manually: scp the 9-file chain + one 'apk add --no-network --allow-untrusted <all files>' — apk resolves fine when every dep is listed as an argument. Fix options (pick one): (a) cheapest — cache-push pushes ALL cache files whose names aren't installed on the node, and pkg_local_install passes every staged .apk for the target's family in one apk add invocation; (b) cleanest — build a local repo index on the node (apk mkndx over stage/pkgs, add as --repository) so --no-network resolves normally; check apk-tools build includes mkndx on OpenWrt 25.12; (c) explicit — keep a dep-closure list next to each entry in the mjolnir-dongle table (it already names the kmods; extend to full closure incl. firmware + usb-core chain). NB wpad-basic/wpad-mesh CONFLICT means 'just add everything staged' is NOT safe without excluding the wpad variants.
close_reason: Fixed with option (a)+(closure walk), verified live on the M3000. Two halves: (1) install-node.sh now pushes the WHOLE cache diff to the node's stage (every cached file not already staged — 46 files on first run), not just $MISSING packages; staging is harmless, only named packages get installed. (2) mjolnir-apply pkg_local_install now computes the staged-file dependency CLOSURE: pkg_file_deps parses 'apk adbdump <file>' depends: (strips version constraints, skips !conflicts and so:/cmd: pseudo-deps), pkg_closure_files walks breadth-first skipping already-installed deps, and apk gets ALL needed files in ONE 'add --no-network --allow-untrusted' (apk resolves fine among files handed as args). Degrades gracefully: adbdump absent -> empty deps -> old single-file behavior; opkg branch left single-file (deps-free cases only, noted in comment). Option (b) local repo index is IMPOSSIBLE on OpenWrt 25.12: the trimmed apk-tools has no mkndx/index subcommand (verified on hardware). LIVE VALIDATION: removed kmod-rt2800-usb + kmod-rt2800-lib from the M3000, re-ran install-node.sh — closure log shows exactly the two needed files, both reinstalled offline, result OK, no WARN, wifi untouched. Stub harness (3 scenarios) still green.

title: Fleet plug-and-play USB wifi dongles: supported-hardware table + auto-config (mjolnir-dongle)
description: Any supported USB dongle plugged into any fleet node auto-configures: hotplug handler on running nodes, mjolnir-dongle apply during setup-wireless.sh and mjolnir-apply. Table (vid:pid -> kmods -> role) in files/usr/sbin/mjolnir-dongle is single source of truth; install-node.sh preinstalls all supported drivers fleet-wide (apk fetch -R for deps) so field nodes need no internet at plug time. Role ap2g = dedicated 2.4GHz Lightning Mesh client AP on br-lan (oaq workaround). Validated end-to-end on TR3000 (RT5370): apply, idempotent re-run, hotplug events during wifi reload, health gate passed.
close_reason: Closed

title: remote node management + OTA updates over the overlay (IdentiKey-authenticated)
description: Product path: a box someone plugs in should auto-join (802.11s locally, iroh over internet egress) and be remotely manageable by its OWNER without ethernet or being on-site. Building blocks: (1) the detached applier (see in-band node ops bead) becomes the OTA agent — same snapshot/apply/health-gate/rollback, but the payload arrives over an iroh stream instead of scp; (2) payloads signed + authorization via IdentiKey key-based auth (implementation not yet complete — blocker); (3) node advertises version/health via the CRDT peer announcement (buw.9), giving the maintainer a live fleet inventory (mjolnirctl nodes) instead of a hand-kept list; (4) maintainer-local node list stays as the bootstrap/recovery inventory. Explicitly NOT mDNS (does not cross br-lan/br-mesh without reflectors, dies in L3-routed 0yb future and cross-site). Depends on: IdentiKey auth, buw.9.

title: Wire new node 2b26d886 into fleet peer lists (TR3000/M3000 offline during bring-up)
description: New Cudy box provisioned 2026-07-01 at bench (192.168.1.1): install-node.sh + setup-wireless.sh run, meshd up on br-mesh (10.254.12.214), node id 2b26d88630e0989625e2cf0d2c980d9e114d0566022d0005cac83056ad73d250. TR3000 (06318b42...) added as its peer.
